snippets.title: Agglomerative clustering with scikit-learn
snippets.code:
from sklearn.cluster import AgglomerativeClustering
import numpy as np

X = np.array([[1, 2], [1.5, 1.8], [5, 8], [8, 8], [1, 0.6], [9, 11]])

model = AgglomerativeClustering(
    n_clusters=2,
    metric="euclidean",
    linkage="ward",
)
labels = model.fit_predict(X)
print(labels)
snippets.explanation: `AgglomerativeClustering` merges bottom-up. `linkage='ward'` minimizes variance increases and requires Euclidean distance. `n_clusters` corresponds to cutting the dendrogram to k groups.

snippets.title: Plot a dendrogram with SciPy
snippets.code:
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import linkage, dendrogram
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

X = np.array([[1, 2], [1.5, 1.8], [5, 8], [8, 8], [1, 0.6], [9, 11]])
Z = linkage(pdist(X, metric="euclidean"), method="ward")

dendrogram(Z, labels=["A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F"])
plt.ylabel("merge distance")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: `linkage` builds the merge tree; `dendrogram` visualizes it. The y-axis shows dissimilarity at each merge—useful for choosing where to cut.

snippets.title: Compare linkage methods on the same data
snippets.code:
from scipy.cluster.hierarchy import linkage, fcluster
from scipy.spatial.distance import pdist
import numpy as np

X = np.random.randn(30, 2)
D = pdist(X, metric="euclidean")

for method in ["single", "complete", "average", "ward"]:
    Z = linkage(D, method=method)
    labels = fcluster(Z, t=3, criterion="maxclust")
    print(method, np.bincount(labels))
snippets.explanation: `fcluster` cuts the tree to a fixed number of clusters (`maxclust`). Different linkage methods often yield different label counts and shapes even with the same k.
